articles.title: S&P 500 futures are little changed as traders assess rising tensions between the U.S. and Iran: Live updates - CNBC
articles.description: The U.S. launched fresh strikes on Iran, after President Donald Trump said earlier in the day he may no longer be interested in negotiating a deal.

articles.title: First Freedom Fuel gas station opens as Trump-backed discounts roll out - Fox Business
articles.description: The White House says the first Freedom Fuel gas station has opened in Philadelphia, offering gasoline for about 50 cents below the statewide average as part of a broader rollout.

articles.title: State AGs finalizing antitrust lawsuit over Paramount–Warner Bros. Discovery merger - CNN
articles.description: Lawyers for several states are finalizing an antitrust lawsuit challenging Paramount’s pending acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery, including CNN, according to people involved in the legal process.

articles.title: John Deere owners will get the right to repair their own equipment under a new FTC settlement - AP News

articles.title: Despite ‘misgivings,’ judge approves Elon Musk’s $1.5M SEC settlement - TechCrunch
articles.description: The saga of Musk's tussle with the SEC over how he disclosed his growing stake in Twitter (now X) has come to an end.

articles.title: Meta to Build First Data Center in Canada, Expanding Global Fleet - Bloomberg.com
articles.description: Meta Platforms Inc. will invest around $10 billion to build its first data center in Canada as the company expands its infrastructure to support its artificial intelligence ambitions.

articles.title: Sun Valley’s ‘summer camp for billionaires’ is offering knitting classes this year — here’s why we shouldn’t be surprised - New York Post
articles.description: It was as if the bankers at Allen & Co. had set out to prove that big media as we know it is on its last legs.

articles.title: Scoop: SpaceXAI launches new model, Grok 4.5 - Axios

articles.title: Labor force participation falls to 61.5%, the lowest in 50 years outside COVID, and economists say it’s not just people giving up - Yahoo Finance
articles.description: Although AI and tech innovation is blamed for that drop, it has more to do with the lack of supply relative to the number of jobs that are available.

articles.title: Meta AI now lets people make deepfakes from public Instagram photos without explicit consent - NBC News
articles.description: Meta’s new Muse AI can generate realistic images based on public Instagram images, including images of many celebrities and public figures.

articles.title: Zuckerberg Rips Damages Sought In Social Media Lawsuit By California, Others - Deadline
articles.description: Meta has trashed the $1.4 trillion In damages that California and other states want from the social media platform in their safety lawsuit.

articles.title: Delta Offers a Cheaper Way to Fly First Class With Fewer Perks - Bloomberg.com
articles.description: Delta Air Lines Inc. is bringing the basic-economy playbook to the front of the plane, offering cheaper, more restrictive fares for first class and other premium cabins.

articles.title: Should you sign your kids up for Trump Accounts? Four things to consider - NPR
articles.description: Families can still benefit even if their children aren't eligible for the free $1,000 contribution from the federal government.

articles.title: Waymo will go autonomous in Denver later this month - Denverite

articles.title: Threads has a problem with people cross-posting from Facebook and Instagram — and not realizing it - Business Insider
articles.description: Threads is full of people who seem to be accidentally crossposting from Instagram or Facebook, like the "This is Richard's wife" woman.

articles.title: Micron Stock Tumbles Further After Entering Bear Market - Barron's
articles.description: Micron stock is in a technical bear market, meaning it is more than 20% down from its recent closing high.
